description: Vectorized NumPy array access for clump attributes.

functions.name: damp
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the clump local damping.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.clumparray.damp()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.

examples.code:

examples.code:

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
This function is version-specific within the supported PFC 6.0/7.0/9.0 documentation set.

functions.name: extra
functions.signature: itasca.clumparray.extra (slot : int) -> array float{clump} or float{clump,3}.
functions.description: Get the clump extra data in the given slot as an array. Extra variables accessed by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.clumparray.extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

functions.name: fill_damp
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the clump local damping. The array must be the correct shape.

examples.code:
import numpy as np
import itasca as it
data = np.empty_like(it.clumparray.damp())
it.clumparray.fill_damp(data)
examples.description: Fill a preallocated NumPy array to avoid allocating a new result array.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
The destination array must already have the correct shape and dtype.

functions.name: set_damp

examples.code:

Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_extra
functions.signature: itasca.clumparray.set_extra (slot: int, data: array float{clump} or float{clump, 3}) -> None.
functions.description: Set the clump extra data in the given slot with an array. Extra variables set by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.clumparray.set_extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

functions.name: set_group
functions.signature: itasca.clumparray.set_group (membership: array bool{clump}, group_name: str, slot=1) -> None.
functions.description: Set clump group from an array. Where membership True set the corresponding clump to be a member of group group_name in the given slot.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.clumparray.set_group()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_moi_prin_real
functions.description: Set the clump principal moments of inertia from an array. When modified no other clump attributes are changed (e.g., pebble sizes, clump volume, etc.). The specification of the moment of inertia in this way results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a clump unless the user changes the fix state.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.clumparray.set_moi_prin_real()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_pos
functions.description: Set the clump centroid location from an array.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
positions = it.clumparray.pos()
positions[:, 2] += 0.1
it.clumparray.set_pos(positions)
examples.description: Modify positions in bulk and write them back.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_vol
functions.description: Set the clump volume from an array. In 2D this is the volume per unit thickness. When modified no other clump attributes are changed (e.g., moment of inertia, pebble sizes, etc.). The specification of the volume in this way results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a clump unless the user changes the fix state.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.clumparray.set_vol()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
